title: Festivals & events
subtitle: 40 celebrations through the year — pick a day to see what's on.

festivals.name: New Year's Day
festivals.description: National public holiday marking the start of the new year celebrated across Colombia with family gatherings and festivities.

festivals.name: Feria de Manizales
festivals.description: One of Colombia's most important festivals featuring bullfighting, concerts, parades, and cultural events in the heart of coffee country. A vibrant celebration of local traditions and international performances.

festivals.name: Epiphany
festivals.description: Religious holiday celebrating the visit of the Three Kings, observed with parades and family traditions throughout Colombia.

festivals.name: Festival de Música Religiosa de Popayán
festivals.description: A prestigious international religious music festival showcasing classical and sacred music performances in the historic city of Popayán.

festivals.name: Carnival of Whites and Blacks (Carnival de Negros y Blancos)
festivals.description: UNESCO-recognized carnival with colonial roots where participants paint each other with white powder and black tar, a unique and joyful celebration of cultural diversity. One of South America's most distinctive carnivals.

festivals.name: Saint Joseph's Day
festivals.description: Religious holiday honoring Saint Joseph, celebrated with processions and traditional family gatherings across Colombia.

festivals.name: Semana Santa (Holy Week)
festivals.description: Major religious observance throughout Colombia featuring processions, passion plays, and religious services. Particularly notable in Popayán with its elaborate traditional processions.

festivals.name: Feria de las Flores (Flower Festival)
festivals.description: Medellín's largest festival celebrating the city's famous flowers with flower displays, parades, concerts, and cultural performances. A vibrant celebration of renewal and local pride.

festivals.name: Festival del Huila
festivals.description: Regional cultural festival in Neiva featuring music, dance, food, and crafts celebrating Huilense traditions and heritage.

festivals.name: Corpus Christi
festivals.description: Religious holiday observed with processions and masses throughout Colombia, with particularly elaborate celebrations in highland regions.

festivals.name: Sacred Heart Festival
festivals.description: Religious observance celebrated with processions and religious services throughout Colombia.

festivals.name: Saint Peter and Saint Paul's Day
festivals.description: National public holiday honoring the two saints with religious processions and traditional celebrations.

festivals.name: Assumption of Mary
festivals.description: National religious holiday celebrating the Assumption with processions and mass throughout Colombia.

festivals.name: Fiesta del Mar
festivals.description: Coastal celebration in Santa Marta featuring water sports, music, dance, and beach festivities honoring the Caribbean Sea.

festivals.name: Festival de la Leyenda Vallenata
festivals.description: Prestigious international festival celebrating vallenato music with competitions, concerts, and performances in Valledupar. Essential for understanding Colombian Caribbean music culture.

festivals.name: All Saints' Day
festivals.description: National holiday when Colombians honor deceased loved ones with cemetery visits and family gatherings.

festivals.name: All Souls' Day
festivals.description: Observed day for remembering deceased souls with traditional cemetery visits and family celebrations throughout Colombia.

festivals.name: Independence Day
festivals.description: National holiday celebrating Colombia's independence from Spain with parades, speeches, and patriotic celebrations across the country.

festivals.name: Battle of Boyacá
festivals.description: National holiday commemorating the decisive 1819 battle that secured Colombian independence, celebrated with patriotic events and ceremonies.

festivals.name: Immaculate Conception
festivals.description: Major Catholic feast day marking the beginning of Christmas season with religious services and celebrations throughout Colombia.

festivals.name: Christmas Day
festivals.description: National holiday celebrating the birth of Jesus Christ with family gatherings, religious services, and festive traditions.

festivals.name: Feria de Cali
festivals.description: Cali's famous year-end festival celebrating salsa music, dance, and Caribbean culture with concerts, parades, and street performances. The world's salsa capital comes alive with energy.

festivals.name: Festival Folclórico Colombiano
festivals.description: Major cultural festival celebrating Colombian folklore, traditional music, dance, and crafts with performances from across the country.

festivals.name: Barranquilla Carnival
festivals.description: One of South America's largest carnivals featuring elaborate floats, costumes, music, and dancing celebrating African and Caribbean heritage. A world-renowned spectacle of color and rhythm.

festivals.name: Festival de la Canción (Song Festival)
festivals.description: Annual music competition and cultural festival in Medellín featuring emerging and established Colombian artists.

festivals.name: Fiesta de San Pacho
festivals.description: Traditional festival in Quibdó celebrating Saint Francis with religious processions, music, and local cultural expressions of the Pacific coast.

festivals.name: Festival del Bambuco
festivals.description: Cultural festival in Neiva celebrating the bambuco, a traditional Andean music and dance form, with competitions and performances.

festivals.name: Feria de Ibagué
festivals.description: Annual fair in Ibagué featuring music competitions, cultural performances, and celebrations of local traditions.

festivals.name: Festival de Música Electrónica
festivals.description: Modern music festival in Cartagena attracting international electronic and dance music artists to the historic Caribbean coastal city.

festivals.name: Festa de la Pitanza
festivals.description: Regional festival celebrating llanero (plains) culture with traditional music, dancing, and food in the grasslands of eastern Colombia.

festivals.name: Feria Equina de Manizales
festivals.description: Equestrian fair in Manizales featuring horse shows, competitions, and celebrations of the region's livestock heritage during Feria de Manizales.

festivals.name: Festival de Gastronomía y Turismo
festivals.description: Food and tourism festival celebrating Colombian cuisine and local culinary traditions with tastings and cooking demonstrations.

festivals.name: Festival del Cacao
festivals.description: Festival celebrating cacao production and chocolate-making traditions in the Amazon region with tastings and cultural activities.

festivals.name: Fiesta de los Invisibles
festivals.description: Festive celebration in Bogotá on Holy Innocents' Day with playful pranks and comedic parades poking fun at public figures.

festivals.name: Festival de Música Indígena
festivals.description: Festival celebrating indigenous music and cultures of the Sierra Nevada with performances and cultural exchanges.

festivals.name: Petronio Álvarez Music Festival
festivals.description: Prestigious festival in Cali celebrating Pacific coast music styles including currulao, salsa, and traditional African-Colombian rhythms.

festivals.name: Feria de Manizales Desfile de Autos
festivals.description: Car parade and automobile exhibition during Feria de Manizales showcasing classic and modern vehicles.

festivals.name: Festival de la Cumbia
festivals.description: Festival celebrating cumbia, Colombia's most iconic music genre, with competitions, traditional performances, and cultural exhibitions.

festivals.name: Feria de Bogotá
festivals.description: Bogotá's major annual fair featuring art exhibitions, concerts, theater, and cultural performances celebrating the capital's diverse culture.

festivals.name: Festival de Artes Vivas
festivals.description: Biennial international festival in Bogotá showcasing contemporary theater, dance, performance art, and multimedia productions.
